explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short option positions have positive theta — they benefit from time decay. Long positions have negative theta — they lose value daily from time decay. **The Trap:** Theta is 'friendly to the option seller' as stated in the workbook. **Cross-Exam Context:** Theta's directional impact is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option sellers often say 'time is on my side' — every day that passes erodes the buyer's position value, all else equal.
